title: CI/API timestamps are UTC — read as local time, events were misdated by a day
summary: gh/CI output printed 15:2x UTC; the local clock said 00:2x next day. The agent concluded a merge was 'yesterday' when it happened minutes earlier, and nearly misdiagnosed live concurrent activity as history.
mistake: Reasoning about recency ('this ran yesterday', 'nothing is active now') directly from raw timestamps in gh run list, API responses, or CI logs. Those are UTC; for a UTC+9 operator every evening event lands on the previous calendar date, so causality and concurrency judgments come out wrong.
instead: Convert to local time before any recency or ordering claim (UTC+offset; JST = UTC+9). Heuristic: if timeline conclusions look about 9 hours or one day off from expectations, check the timezone first. Prefer sources that render locally: `git log --date=iso-local`, `date -d '<ts>'`. State timestamps with their zone in reports.
